corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

Detailed dataset statistics are provided in Table .
Furthermore, existing methods are ineffective to capture the temporal trends as the RNNs or CNNs employed in these methods cannot capture long-range temporal sequences.
To overcome these limitations, we propose in this paper a novel graph neural network architecture, Graph WaveNet, for spatial-temporal graph modeling.
To overcome these limitations, we propose in this paper a novel graph neural network architecture, Graph WaveNet, for spatial-temporal graph modeling.
These two components are integrated seamlessly in a unified framework and the whole framework is learned in an end-to-end manner.
Experimental results on two public traffic network datasets, METR-LA and PEMS-BAY, demonstrate the superior performance of our algorithm.
Therefore how to capture spatial and temporal dependencies simultaneously becomes a primary challenge.
In this work, we present a CNN-based method named Graph WaveNet, which addresses the two shortcomings we have aforementioned.
We propose a graph convolution layer in which a self-adaptive adjacency matrix can be learned from the data through an end-to-end supervised training.

Experiments validate that our method improves the results when spatial dependencies are known to exist but are not provided. • We present an effective and efficient framework to capture spatial-temporal dependencies simultaneously.

Experiments validate that our method improves the results when spatial dependencies are known to exist but are not provided. • We present an effective and efficient framework to capture spatial-temporal dependencies simultaneously.

The core idea is to assemble our proposed graph convolution with dilated casual convolution in a way that each graph convolution layer tackles spatial dependencies of nodes' information extracted by dilated casual convolution layers at different granular levels. • We evaluate our proposed model on traffic datasets and achieve state-of-the-art results with low computation costs.
We think this is because our architecture is more capable of detecting spatial dependencies at each temporal stage.
